surface_tension._note: from the R1132A.FLD file in the Supporting Information; fit by M.L. Huber to proprietary data from R.E. Low (Orbia); estimated uncertainty (k=2) 0.3 mN/m

crit_anc._note: coefficients are for the function like ln(|rho^A-rhoc|) = sum_i c_i ln(Theta)^i with Theta=(Tc-T)/Tc

conductivity._note: ECS parameters (R134a reference) from the R1132A.FLD file in the Supporting Information of doi:10.1007/s10765-026-03794-8, fit by M.L. Huber to proprietary data from Orbia Fluor and Energy Materials; estimated uncertainty 6% (gas), 1% (liquid). Critical enhancement uses CoolProp default simplified Olchowy-Sengers parameters.

viscosity._note: ECS parameters (R134a reference) from the R1132A.FLD file in the Supporting Information of doi:10.1007/s10765-026-03794-8, fit by M.L. Huber to proprietary data from Orbia Fluor and Energy Materials; estimated uncertainty 2% (liquid and gas). Lennard-Jones parameters estimated with the method of Chung et al. (1988): sigma = 0.4333 nm, epsilon/k = 240.31 K. Because CoolProp's ECS does not implement REFPROP's BIG (large-molecule) dilute-gas correction, the constant BIG factor of 0.934 is folded into an effective sigma_eta = 0.4333/sqrt(0.934) nm, which multiplies the kinetic-theory dilute-gas viscosity (used by both the viscosity and thermal conductivity ECS models) by exactly 0.934; verified against REFPROP 10 with this FLD (agreement <0.5% in liquid and dilute gas; residual deviations up to ~4% at moderate vapor densities stem from CoolProp's ECS lacking the initial-density Rainwater-Friend correction).
